title: fix: wan-pro image-to-video failing with 500
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
wan-pro (wan2.7) image-to-video has been failing every request with a 500.

- Root cause: wan2.7 dropped the legacy `input.img_url` string for a unified `input.media[]` array (`{type, url}`). The handler still sent `img_url` — DashScope accepts it at submit but fails the task async with "Field required: input.media".
- Fix: wan2.7 i2v now maps the start image to `media:[{type:"first_frame", url}]`; wan2.2/2.6 keep `img_url` (unchanged).
- Also masks `media` URLs in the safe request logger.
- Tests: added i2v schema tests for wan-pro (media array) and wan-fast (legacy img_url regression guard). 4/4 wan tests pass, biome clean.
- Verified end-to-end through the gen worker: wan-pro i2v returns video/mp4 (was 500).

gist.headline: Unjamming the video reels
gist.blurb: Fixed a pesky bug that was causing Wan-Pro image-to-video generations to fail. Your pixels are ready to move again!
gist.summary: Fixed a 500 error in Wan-Pro (wan2.7) image-to-video generation by updating the payload to use the new `input.media[]` array format instead of the legacy `img_url` string.
gist.impact: Users can once again successfully generate videos from images using the Wan-Pro model without encountering server errors.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a jammed film reel or video player, with the monitor robot cheering as the video starts playing smoothly again.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot using a tiny wrench to fix a glowing film reel attached to a retro computer monitor. The monitor screen shows a smooth, moving landscape, glowing in soft lime green (#ecf874). A chunky pixel robot companion cheers with square pixel eyes. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
